coaching.coaching: Pin down contract-violation sink in code-review pipeline with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: contract-violation sink in code-review pipeline

coaching.coaching: Plan how AC1 observable form for outcome_counts surfacing will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: AC1 observable form for outcome_counts surfacing

coaching.coaching: Deliver plan_review re-wiring and new code-review test coverage with existing machinery first; make the ideal version a dependent follow-on ticket.
coaching.subject: plan_review re-wiring and new code-review test coverage

coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance-criteria completeness and verifiability with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance-criteria completeness and verifiability

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Remediation that the only viable path is extracting the outcome_counts tallying logic from `verify_findings` into a shared helper (not re-invoking verify_findings, which would re-execute the LLM call), and describe that helper's signature.
evidence: verify_findings signature: `def verify_findings(findings, *, context, run_chunk: RunChunk, window_tokens, est_tokens, headroom)` — run_chunk is the live LLM seam, not a post-hoc consumer
Both decide ops receive `raw_verifs = list(ctx.inputs.get('verifications') or [])` — verifications already produced by a prior workflow prompt step
Plan remediation says 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings() (or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)'
RunChunk = Callable[[str, str], list[dict[str, Any]]] at verify.py:278 — it IS the LLM call
findings.finding: The plan's primary remediation framing — 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings()' — is architecturally incorrect: verify_findings() requires an injected run_chunk: RunChunk LLM seam and re-executes the LLM call, but both _decide ops receive verifications as already-completed raw_verifs inputs (the workflow prompt step ran previously). Directly calling verify_findings() in either decide op would issue a second LLM call. The plan's own parenthetical '(or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)' is the only viable path, but it is relegated to a parenthetical and the primary framing is misleading.
findings.location: Remediation section; verify.py:281-363 (verify_findings signature requires run_chunk: RunChunk); code_review/workflow_ops.py:395-450 (code_review_decide receives raw_verifs as pre-completed input); plan_review/workflow_ops.py:425-455 (same pattern)
scenarios: If an implementer takes the primary framing literally and calls verify_findings() inside code_review_decide, a second LLM call will be issued per decide invocation, doubling cost and latency.
If the parenthetical path (extract tallying logic) is taken, it requires a new shared helper that does not yet exist in verify.py — this is a creation task the plan should describe explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the missing edit target `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (must activate a run-scoped violation sink for code-review), and specify whether the contract-violation sink will be promoted to a shared kernel utility or duplicated in code_review — the plan currently leaves this scope undefined.
evidence: plan_review/orchestrator.py:341 defines record_contract_violation; gate_dispatch.py:129 imports collect_contract_violations from plan_review.orchestrator and activates it at line 171 only for the plan-review path
_run_code_review_gate (gate_dispatch.py:592) has no equivalent collect_contract_violations scope
src/rebar/llm/code_review/ directory listing: no orchestrator.py module exists
AC2: 'records contract violations via the same mechanism plan_review_decide uses (or a documented equivalent)' — 'documented equivalent' is undefined
findings.finding: The code-review pipeline has no equivalent of plan_review's `orchestrator.record_contract_violation` / `collect_contract_violations()` infrastructure, and `gate_dispatch.py`'s `_run_code_review_gate` does not establish any run-scoped violation sink. AC2 requires `code_review_decide` to 'record contract violations via the same mechanism plan_review_decide uses (or a documented equivalent)', but neither names what that equivalent is nor notes that it must be created — leaving the creation scope and the necessary gate_dispatch.py activation unspecified.
findings.location: AC2; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py (_run_code_review_gate, line 592); src/rebar/llm/code_review/ (no orchestrator module)
scenarios: An implementer adds record_contract_violation calls to code_review_decide but the violation records are silently discarded because no sink scope is active in _run_code_review_gate, giving the appearance of working tests but no production telemetry.
The creation of a code-review contract-violation sink (new module or shared kernel sink) is implicitly out-of-scope but is actually required for AC2 to be satisfiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify WHERE outcome_counts is to be computed: the full tally (including `empty_outcomes` and `unrecoverable`) requires per-chunk execution signals only available in the verify-input/verify-execution phase (before `_merge_chunked_outputs` flattens the output), not in `*_decide`; specify whether the plan means to move the tally to `*_verify_inputs`, emit per-chunk metadata as a separate workflow output, or accept a reduced tally (clean/recovered only) from the reshaped flat list in `*_decide`.
evidence: verify_findings() signature: `run_chunk: RunChunk, window_tokens, est_tokens` — it calls run_chunk in a loop (verify.py:317-334); *_decide steps have no access to run_chunk or the per-chunk model call
code-review.yaml:160: `verifications: ${{ steps.verify.outputs.verifications }}` — the decide step receives the already-merged flat list from the verify prompt step, not per-chunk outputs
runs.py:106: 'list-valued fields are CONCATENATED in chunk order (e.g. the verifier's `verifications`, re-merged by their global `index`)' — per-chunk structure is gone by the time *_decide runs
outcome_counts computation at verify.py:314 happens inside the run_chunk loop; empty_outcomes (line 341) and unrecoverable (line 330) are chunk-level signals with no equivalent in a flat merged list

Plan remediation: 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings() (or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)' — the 'or' fallback is underspecified and the primary option is unworkable from the decide step
findings.finding: The plan's primary mechanism — wiring `*_decide` to call `verify_findings()` — is architecturally incompatible with the workflow step boundary: both `plan_review_decide` and `code_review_decide` receive a pre-merged flat `verifications` list from the workflow runner (`_merge_chunked_outputs`), not raw per-chunk outputs, so `verify_findings()` (which owns the `run_chunk` loop and computes `outcome_counts` per chunk during execution) cannot be called from the decide step. The per-chunk signals `empty_outcomes` (chunk returned empty despite input) and `unrecoverable` (StructuredOutputError per chunk) are irrecoverably lost after the workflow merges chunk outputs.
scenarios: Implementer attempts to call verify_findings() from code_review_decide — fails because there is no run_chunk callable in that scope

Implementer falls back to 'reuse tallying logic' in *_decide but can only reconstruct clean/recovered (from the merged flat list via reshape_verifications), losing empty_outcomes and unrecoverable counts entirely
outcome_counts in the final verdict shows clean=N, recovered=0, empty_outcomes=0, unrecoverable=0 even when chunks silently failed — the telemetry gap persists for two of the four buckets
findings.suggested_fix: Move outcome_counts computation to `*_verify_inputs` (or a new `*_verify_tally` step that wraps the verify prompt step's per-chunk outputs before merging). Emit `outcome_counts` and `contract_violations` as separate named outputs of that step, then thread them into `*_coach` (for coverage recording) rather than `*_decide`. Alternatively, scope AC1 to 'clean/recovered from reshape' only (achievable in `*_decide` from the flat list) and explicitly accept that `empty_outcomes`/`unrecoverable` require a later phase change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete propagation path for `outcome_counts` and contract violations out of `code_review_decide` into the code-review verdict coverage — either define the 'documented equivalent' sink (e.g. a new ContextVar in code_review, a direct output key from the decide step, or a write in `finalize_code_review_verdict`) or state that `coverage['verification_contract_violations']` is the target key and name the call site that writes it.
evidence: AC2 text: 'or a documented equivalent' — the equivalent is never defined anywhere in the plan
plan_review/orchestrator.py:327-358: collect_contract_violations / record_contract_violation / drain_contract_violations — a ContextVar-backed, three-part protocol with no code-review analogue
gate_dispatch.py:171: 'with assemble_context_cache(), collect_contract_violations(), gate_config(cfg)' — only activated for plan-review; _run_code_review_gate has no such context manager
finalize_code_review_verdict in code_review/finalize.py: no drain_contract_violations call, no 'verification_contract_violations' key written to coverage
code_review/workflow_ops.py:401-407: code_review_decide logs contract violations at ERROR only — no record call, consistent with the Finding section of the plan
plan_review/workflow_ops.py:563-565: drain_contract_violations() → coverage['verification_contract_violations'] — the plan-review pattern an executor would need to mirror
findings.finding: AC2 references a 'documented equivalent' mechanism for code_review_decide to record contract violations, but the plan never defines what that equivalent is or where the violation data flows in the code-review pipeline. Unlike plan-review — which has a ContextVar-backed collect_contract_violations context manager activated in gate_dispatch, a record_contract_violation call in plan_review_decide, and a drain_contract_violations call writing to verdict coverage — the code-review pipeline has no analogous sink, no context manager in _run_code_review_gate, and no drain path in finalize_code_review_verdict. An executor reading only the plan cannot determine how to propagate outcome_counts or contract violations out of code_review_decide into the verdict coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'code_review_decide records contract violations via the same mechanism plan_review_decide uses (or a documented equivalent)'
scenarios: An executor wires code_review_decide to call verify_findings() and propagates outcome_counts, but doesn't know whether to: (a) add a new ContextVar sink to code_review's orchestrator, (b) reuse plan_review's orchestrator sink, (c) write outcome_counts directly onto the step's output dict, or (d) propagate via finalize_code_review_verdict — all are valid interpretations of 'documented equivalent', leading to divergent implementations.

The code-review verdict's coverage dict never gets a 'verification_contract_violations' key (unlike plan-review), making the telemetry asymmetric even after the wiring change, unless the propagation path is explicitly specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC2 such as: 'The documented equivalent is: `code_review_decide` writes `outcome_counts` and `contract_violations` as output keys on its step result dict; `finalize_code_review_verdict` (or `code_review_coach`) reads them and writes `coverage["verification_contract_violations"]` and `coverage["outcome_counts"]` when non-empty — mirroring the plan-review `drain_contract_violations()` → `coverage` write without requiring a new ContextVar sink.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Remediation section whether `outcome_counts` will be surfaced by (a) moving the verify LLM call upstream so `verify_findings()` can be called before the decide step, (b) emitting per-chunk outcomes from the workflow's verify step and threading them into decide, or (c) deriving a coarser tally from `VerificationReshape` at decide-time — and update AC1 to match the chosen granularity.
evidence: verify_findings() signature (verify.py:281-289): requires `run_chunk: RunChunk`, `window_tokens: int`, `est_tokens: TokenEstimator` — it drives LLM execution, not post-execution reshape

plan_review_decide (workflow_ops.py:425-496) and code_review_decide (workflow_ops.py:395-450) both receive `raw_verifs = list(ctx.inputs.get('verifications') or [])` — a flat list from a completed prior workflow step; they do not hold RunChunk, window_tokens, or est_tokens

outcome_counts is tallied per-chunk inside verify_findings (verify.py:314-347), keyed on each chunk's raw_output; after the workflow merges chunk outputs into a flat list, the per-chunk boundary information is lost
AC4 of the plan: 'No change to either pipeline's decision thresholds, impact functions, or verdict shape'
The parenthetical '(or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)' acknowledges the tension but leaves the actual approach unspecified
findings.finding: The plan's primary remediation verb — 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings()' — is a potential golden-hammer misapplication: verify_findings() requires a RunChunk callable and drives LLM calls itself, but both decide steps are post-LLM steps that receive an already-executed flat verifications list from the workflow engine. Calling verify_findings() at the decide step would force those steps to also accept RunChunk/window_tokens/est_tokens and re-run or re-mock the LLM tier — contradicting AC4 ('no change to … verdict shape') and the workflow split-step architecture. The parenthetical hedge ('or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic') is architecturally correct for this context but is underspecified: the decide steps only have the merged flat list and cannot reconstruct chunk-level clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable tallies without access to per-chunk outputs that the workflow engine has already discarded.
findings.location: Remediation section: 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings()'; AC1: 'outcome_counts (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) from the same shared kernel logic'
scenarios: An implementer attempts to call verify_findings() inside code_review_decide, discovers it requires RunChunk injection, and either re-architects the decide step to drive LLM calls (breaking the workflow split) or silently falls back to a coarser reshape-only approach without updating the plan.

outcome_counts AC is satisfied only at a coarser granularity (e.g., a single 'clean vs has_violations' boolean from VerificationReshape.summary()) rather than the chunk-level clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable the plan promises — causing the AC to pass in spirit but not in letter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC3 sub-requirement for new or updated tests that verify plan_review_decide's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation after its migration to verify_findings(), symmetric with the code-review requirement.
evidence: AC3 specifies new tests for code-review only; plan-review side gets no new test requirement.
Remediation says 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings()' — both are migrated, but test work is asymmetric.

plan_review/workflow_ops.py: plan_review_decide currently calls reshape_verifications() directly — its existing tests may stub at that level and would still pass post-migration even if verify_findings() integration is broken.
findings.finding: AC3 requires new tests only for the code-review pipeline's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation, but plan_review_decide is also being re-wired to call verify_findings() instead of calling reshape_verifications() directly. The AC only says existing plan-review suites must 'continue to pass', which could be satisfied even if the new delegation path in plan_review_decide is never exercised by any test (e.g., existing tests that stub reshape_verifications directly would still pass while the new verify_findings call is silently wrong or ignored).
findings.location: AC3 — 'Existing plan-review and code-review test suites continue to pass; new tests cover the code-review pipeline's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation specifically.'
scenarios: plan_review_decide is re-wired to call verify_findings() but outcome_counts is silently discarded or not recorded; existing tests pass because they stub reshape_verifications at a level that doesn't catch the new delegation; the plan-review pipeline ships with broken telemetry.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3 to: 'New tests cover both the plan-review and code-review pipelines' outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation from verify_findings(), not only code-review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC1 to specify the concrete recording mechanism for outcome_counts (e.g. 'outcome_counts is returned as a named field in the decide result struct AND passed to orchestrator.record_outcome_counts(...)'), matching the specificity of AC2's mechanism reference.
evidence: AC1 text: 'both surface outcome_counts (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) from the same shared kernel logic'
The Remediation section mentions 'outcome_counts is populated and recorded consistently' but does not name the recording mechanism (log? structured field returned from decide? orchestrator.record_*?)
By contrast AC2 names a specific mechanism: 'orchestrator.record_contract_violation(...)'
findings.finding: AC1 uses 'surface outcome_counts' without specifying the observable form — it is unspecified whether the counts must appear in a structured return value, a log record, an orchestrator telemetry call, or some other sink — making the criterion subjective to evaluate.
scenarios: One implementer logs the counts; another stores them in a return struct; a reviewer disagrees whether AC1 is met, since 'surface' is ambiguous.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'both surface outcome_counts … from the same shared kernel logic' with 'both pipelines return outcome_counts as a structured field from the decide function AND record it via [named orchestrator/telemetry call], using the same verify_findings() invocation path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the preferred approach in Remediation (e.g. 'prefer calling verify_findings() directly; the extract alternative is permitted only if a documented reason prevents direct delegation') to eliminate the architectural fork as a decision left to the executor.
evidence: 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings() (or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)'
The two options have different structural implications: calling verify_findings() directly vs. duplicating/extracting its internals are meaningfully different design decisions.
findings.finding: The Remediation section presents two design choices without stating a preference: 'call the shared verify_findings() OR extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic', leaving a real architectural fork (monolithic call vs. extracted helper) to the executor.
scenarios: An executor extracts a new helper rather than calling verify_findings(), creating a third code path that diverges from the existing unit-tested kernel — which may or may not satisfy AC1's 'same shared kernel logic'.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)' with '(direct delegation to verify_findings() is preferred; extraction is permitted only when the pipeline's existing signature makes direct call infeasible, and must be documented in a code comment).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile Remediation and AC1: either tighten Remediation to require calling `verify_findings()` directly (removing the 'or extract/reuse' escape hatch), or relax AC1 to accept a documented equivalent shared utility, so both sections point implementers in the same direction.
evidence: Remediation: 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings() (or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)'
AC1: 'both surface outcome_counts (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) from the same shared kernel logic'
findings.finding: The Remediation permits 'extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic' as an alternative to calling verify_findings() directly, but AC1 requires outcome_counts from 'the same shared kernel logic' — an implementer who duplicates the tallying logic (rather than calling the shared function) would satisfy the Remediation guidance but potentially violate AC1's 'same shared kernel logic' wording, sending implementers in two directions.
scenarios: An implementer copies the tallying loop inline in each pipeline, satisfying the Remediation's 'or extract/reuse' branch but forking the logic — future changes to verify_findings() won't propagate, and AC1's 'same shared kernel logic' requirement is arguably violated.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove '(or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic)' from the Remediation and keep the mandate as 'call the shared verify_findings()'; this aligns with AC1's 'same shared kernel logic' and the ticket's stated goal of uniform telemetry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC1 — e.g. a grep/AST check confirming `verify_findings` is called inside `plan_review_decide` and `code_review_decide`, plus a pytest invocation targeting the new integration tests that assert `outcome_counts` is populated in both pipelines.
evidence: AC1 checkbox: 'plan_review_decide and code_review_decide both surface outcome_counts (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) from the same shared kernel logic'

Evidence section notes: 'outcome_counts telemetry that ticket 1aa8-cdde-4ff6-40d0 shipped is exercised by its own unit tests but is not yet surfaced by either production pipeline' — so existing unit tests pass already, meaning a pytest run alone cannot prove AC1
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for AC1 ('both pipelines surface outcome_counts from the same shared kernel logic'). An executor cannot verify AC1 is met vs. the pre-existing state (unit tests already pass without live-pipeline wiring) without a command that confirms the production call path reaches verify_findings.
scenarios: An agent adds the wiring, existing unit tests remain green (they passed before), but no command distinguishes the pre- from post-wiring state for the production code path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Verified by: `grep -n "verify_findings" src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` returns a match in each file, AND `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_workflow.py tests/unit/test_code_review_workflow.py -k outcome_counts` passes.'

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or sub-criterion requiring at least one integration-level test (or a documented rationale for its absence) that exercises the `verify_findings` code path end-to-end through the plan-review or code-review pipeline without mocking `verify_findings` itself.
evidence: AC3: 'new tests cover the code-review pipeline's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation specifically' — does not specify integration vs. unit, or live vs. mocked

The finding section states the gap is about 'surfacing outcome_counts/contract-violation telemetry uniformly' in production pipelines, but no AC requires a live or integration-level exercise of the new call path
findings.finding: The cutover from inline reshape_verifications calls to the shared verify_findings path in both production pipelines has no end-to-end criterion that exercises the wired code path as it runs in production (i.e., through the live orchestrator, not only mocked unit tests). AC3 specifies 'new tests' but does not require they exercise the live pipeline boundary.
scenarios: New unit tests mock verify_findings and assert the mock was called; tests pass; but if the wiring imports a wrong symbol or the call signature drifts, no live-path criterion catches it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit test-authoring step to the Remediation (or a separate Deliverables section) that describes writing new tests for code_review_decide's outcome_counts and contract-violation propagation, so AC3 has a corresponding described deliverable.
evidence: AC3: 'new tests cover the code-review pipeline's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation specifically'
Remediation: describes only wiring changes ('Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide…'); no mention of writing new tests
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'new tests cover the code-review pipeline's outcome_counts/contract-violation propagation specifically', but the Remediation section lists no test-writing deliverable — test authoring is an uncovered work item with no corresponding description in the plan body.
scenarios: A reviewer or implementer reading only the Remediation section to scope the work would miss the test-authoring deliverable; AC3 then appears as an orphan criterion with no backing description.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Remediation: 'Add unit/integration tests for code_review_decide that assert outcome_counts fields are populated and that reshape violations trigger record_contract_violation, mirroring the existing plan_review test coverage.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the Remediation section explaining which workflow step boundary is the intended integration point for `outcome_counts` tally and why it fits the existing architecture (e.g. 'computed in `*_verify_inputs` because chunking already occurs there and per-chunk signals are available before the workflow merges outputs').
evidence: Remediation reads: 'Wire both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide to call the shared verify_findings() (or extract/reuse its chunk-running + tallying logic) so outcome_counts is populated…' — no rationale for why this boundary was selected

ABSENCE: no explanation of why verify_findings() delegation from *_decide is preferred over computing outcome_counts in *_verify_inputs (where chunking already occurs), or over a separate telemetry step
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for why wiring `verify_findings()` into the decide step is the chosen approach — it describes only what to do, not why this boundary (decide vs verify_inputs vs a new intermediate step) best fits the codebase's workflow-step architecture, blast radius, or testability.
findings.location: Remediation section (plan body, no section header)
scenarios: An implementer chooses the wrong sub-approach (decide vs verify_inputs) because the plan doesn't explain which boundary was intended or why

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update the parent epic (6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9) to add a plan item/AC explicitly covering consumer-pipeline `outcome_counts` telemetry wiring, or re-parent this leaf to a more appropriate container — the leaf's current deliverable is outside the parent's declared scope and must be reconciled before proceeding.
evidence: Parent epic AC3: 'Verification preserves flexible provider-independent parsing and self-healing, but no semantically empty result can be accepted as a valid review outcome.' — covers correctness only, not outcome_counts telemetry wiring.

Parent epic file_impact: lists src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py and src/rebar/llm/verify_models.py, but NOT src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py, src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py, or tests/ — the exact files the leaf modifies.
Epic comment: 'c2c5-53a7-2ce0-4d98 (verify_findings telemetry wiring) was discovered mid-run and linked as a child but was explicitly scoped OUT of this epic execution per standing session decision.'

Leaf AC1: 'plan_review_decide and code_review_decide both surface outcome_counts (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) from the same shared kernel logic.' — no corresponding item in parent ACs.
findings.finding: The leaf's deliverable — wiring `verify_findings`/`outcome_counts` telemetry into `plan_review_decide` and `code_review_decide` at the consumer-pipeline level — is not contained in the parent epic's (6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9) declared plan. The parent's five ACs and file_impact list cover DCO identity, force semantics, verification correctness (empty-outcome rejection), plan-review hierarchy completeness, and ChatGPT connector guidance; none address consumer-pipeline telemetry wiring. The parent's file_impact list does not include `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py` or `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py`. The epic's own comment confirms the leaf was 'explicitly scoped OUT of this epic execution.'
findings.location: Leaf ticket c2c5-53a7-2ce0-4d98 `parent_id: 6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9`; parent epic AC3 and file_impact list
scenarios: A reviewer checking the parent epic's plan finds no authorization for the consumer-pipeline telemetry wiring work the leaf proposes — the leaf is formally a child but delivers something outside the parent's scope.
If the leaf proceeds and is merged, the parent epic's completion review will face a child whose deliverable was never planned in the parent, potentially causing attestation or gate failures.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to the parent epic such as: 'Both plan_review_decide and code_review_decide emit outcome_counts telemetry (clean/recovered/empty_outcomes/unrecoverable) via the shared verify_findings kernel, and code_review_decide records contract violations via the same mechanism as plan_review_decide.' Then update the epic's file_impact to include src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py and src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py. Alternatively, create a new parent story or epic that covers post-1aa8 pipeline wiring and re-parent c2c5 under it.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
